questions.question: The Magna Carta, signed in 1215, established what groundbreaking principle that was later foundational to democratic systems?
options: Universal voting rights for all men
The rule of law — that even kings are subject to law
questions.answer: The rule of law — that even kings are subject to law
questions.explanation: King John of England was forced by rebellious barons to sign the Magna Carta ('Great Charter') in 1215, establishing that the king could not arbitrarily imprison, punish, or tax people without lawful judgment. Clause 39 is considered the foundation of habeas corpus. The document became a model for constitutional limits on government in Britain, the US, and globally.
questions.memory_hook: The Magna Carta (1215) established that kings must follow law — not make it up as they go. It's the ancestor of every constitution and bill of rights in the democratic world.

questions.question: The Louisiana Purchase in 1803 roughly doubled the size of the United States. From which country did President Jefferson purchase it?
questions.explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte sold the Louisiana Territory — approximately 828,000 square miles covering 15 present-day US states — to the United States for $15 million (about 3 cents per acre). Napoleon needed money for his European wars and had given up on a French empire in the Americas after the Haitian Revolution. The purchase extended US territory from the Mississippi River to the Rocky Mountains.
questions.memory_hook: Napoleon sold 828,000 square miles to the US for $15 million — 3 cents per acre — because he needed war funds. The Haitian Revolution killed his dream of a New World French empire.

questions.question: On December 1, 1955, Rosa Parks refused to give up her seat on a Montgomery, Alabama bus. What law was she violating by sitting where she sat?
options: Segregation laws requiring Black passengers to sit at the back
A city ordinance against public protests
questions.answer: Segregation laws requiring Black passengers to sit at the back
questions.explanation: Rosa Parks was seated in the 'colored section' of a Montgomery city bus, but when the 'white section' filled, the driver ordered Black passengers to move further back. Parks refused to give up her seat and was arrested. Her arrest sparked the Montgomery Bus Boycott (381 days), organized by a young Martin Luther King Jr., which ended with the Supreme Court ruling bus segregation unconstitutional.
questions.memory_hook: Rosa Parks refused to move from her legally assigned seat when white passengers needed more room. Her arrest sparked a 381-day boycott that ended bus segregation nationwide.

questions.question: Floyd Mayweather Jr. retired in 2017 with a professional boxing record of 50-0. What made this achievement historically significant?
options: He held world titles in a record 8 different weight classes simultaneously
He retired undefeated with 50 wins, surpassing Rocky Marciano's previous record of 49-0 and spanning five weight classes
He knocked out all 50 opponents without a single fight going to decision
He became the first boxer to win and defend a world title in five different decades
questions.answer: He retired undefeated with 50 wins, surpassing Rocky Marciano's previous record of 49-0 and spanning five weight classes
questions.explanation: Mayweather won world titles at super featherweight, lightweight, super lightweight, welterweight, and super welterweight. His 50th win came in the heavily promoted fight against UFC star Conor McGregor in 2017. Mayweather's defensive style — shoulder roll, counter-punching — was technically masterful, though critics argued he avoided the most dangerous opponents at their peaks. His 50-0 record surpassed Marciano's previously legendary 49-0.
questions.memory_hook: Mayweather's 50-0 (retired 2017) broke Marciano's 49-0 record across five weight classes — his 'shoulder roll' defensive style made him nearly impossible to hit cleanly for 21 years.

questions.question: What significant record did New Zealand's Allyson Felix break at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, making her the most decorated US track and field Olympian?
options: Most total Olympic medals in track and field with 11
First woman to win 5 consecutive Olympic golds
questions.answer: Most total Olympic medals in track and field with 11
questions.explanation: Allyson Felix won her 11th Olympic medal at the Tokyo 2020 Games (held in 2021), surpassing Carl Lewis's record of 10. She has 7 gold, 3 silver, and 1 bronze across five Olympics (2004-2021). Felix is also notable for her advocacy against Nike's maternal policies after her pregnancy in 2018.
questions.memory_hook: Allyson Felix's 11 Olympic medals broke Carl Lewis's record to make her America's most decorated track and field Olympian. She competed at five Games spanning 17 years.

questions.question: The 1939 film 'The Wizard of Oz' featured which iconic song that won the Academy Award for Best Original Song?
options: We're Off to See the Wizard
Ding Dong the Witch Is Dead
questions.explanation: Judy Garland's performance of 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' won the 1940 Academy Award for Best Original Song. The song was almost cut from the film because studio executives thought it slowed down the pacing. The American Film Institute later named it the greatest song in American cinema history. Garland was 16 years old when she recorded it.
questions.memory_hook: Judy Garland recorded 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' at 16, and studios almost cut it from the film. AFI later named it the greatest song in film history.

questions.question: Which musician, born in Mississippi in 1935, is often credited as the 'King of Rock and Roll' and had his first major hit with 'That's All Right' in 1954?
questions.explanation: Elvis Presley recorded 'That's All Right' at Sun Studio in Memphis in July 1954. He blended Black R&B and gospel music with country in a way that was culturally revolutionary — and controversial. By 1956 he was a global superstar with a string of hits including 'Heartbreak Hotel,' 'Hound Dog,' and 'Jailhouse Rock.' He served in the US Army 1958-60.
questions.memory_hook: Elvis's 1954 Sun Studio recordings blended Black R&B with country in a way that created rock and roll. His moves were so provocative that early TV shows filmed him only from the waist up.

questions.question: F. Scott Fitzgerald published 'The Great Gatsby' in 1925. The novel is set in which decade and is considered a defining portrait of what era?
questions.explanation: The Great Gatsby (1925) is set in the summer of 1922 and is widely considered the defining novel of the 1920s Jazz Age — a period of rapid economic growth, social upheaval, and cultural change following WWI. The novel sold poorly on publication and Fitzgerald died believing it a failure. It was only recognized as a masterpiece after his death in 1940.
questions.memory_hook: Fitzgerald published Gatsby in 1925, set in the Roaring Twenties of parties, wealth, and the American Dream's dark underside. He died thinking it had failed — it's now one of the most-read novels in English.

questions.question: The Strait of Gibraltar, which separates Europe from Africa, connects which two bodies of water?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar is only about 14 km (9 miles) wide at its narrowest point and separates Spain (and Gibraltar) from Morocco. It is the only natural connection between the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea, making it one of the world's most strategically important waterways. The name comes from the Arabic 'Jabal al-Tariq' (mountain of Tariq) after the Moorish commander who crossed in 711.
questions.memory_hook: Gibraltar is just 14 km wide between Spain and Morocco — Europe and Africa almost touching. The Arabic name 'Jabal al-Tariq' (Tariq's mountain) honors the Moorish general who crossed it in 711.

questions.question: The Scandinavian Peninsula is home to which three countries?
questions.explanation: The Scandinavian Peninsula is occupied by Norway on its western and northern coast, Sweden on its eastern side, and Finland to the northeast. The peninsula extends from the Barents Sea in the north to Denmark in the south (though Denmark is often culturally grouped with Scandinavia, it sits on the Jutland Peninsula). The peninsula is bordered by the North Sea, Norwegian Sea, Barents Sea, and Baltic Sea.
questions.memory_hook: Norway (west coast) and Sweden (east) share the main Scandinavian Peninsula; Finland sits to the northeast. Despite being culturally Scandinavian, Denmark is technically on the separate Jutland Peninsula.

questions.question: Newton's Second Law states that Force = Mass × Acceleration. What does this mean practically?
options: Objects in motion stay in motion
Heavier objects need more force to accelerate at the same rate
Every force has an equal and opposite reaction
questions.answer: Heavier objects need more force to accelerate at the same rate
questions.explanation: F=ma means that to achieve the same acceleration, a more massive object requires proportionally more force. Pushing a shopping cart vs. pushing a car: same push, very different acceleration. It also explains why rockets need enormous thrust (force) to accelerate their massive fuel load. And why a bullet fired from a gun (small mass) travels faster than the gun recoils (large mass).
questions.memory_hook: F=ma: double the mass, need double the force for the same acceleration. That's why a loaded truck needs a much more powerful engine than an empty one.

questions.question: The pH scale measures acidity and alkalinity. Which pH value is neutral, and what do values below 7 indicate?
questions.explanation: The pH scale runs from 0 (most acidic) to 14 (most alkaline/basic), with 7 (pure water) as neutral. The scale is logarithmic — pH 5 is 10× more acidic than pH 6. Battery acid is ~0, stomach acid is ~2, coffee ~5, blood ~7.4, baking soda ~9, bleach ~12. Acids donate hydrogen ions (H⁺); bases accept them. The pH of blood is tightly regulated — even small deviations can be fatal.
questions.memory_hook: pH 7 = neutral (pure water). Below 7 = acidic (stomach acid is pH 2); above 7 = alkaline (bleach is pH 12). The scale is logarithmic — each step is 10x.
